content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and TTT. 

Find the length of the largest string KKK such that is is a subsequence of both SSS and TTT.


Input Format

First line of the input contains the string SSS.
Second line of the input contains the string TTT.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum size of the subsequence that is common in SSS and TTT.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains string S, the second line contains string T.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the length of the longest common subsequence of the two strings.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The LCS of "code" and "chef" is "ce", length 2.
No common characters, so LCS length is 0.
The two strings share no characters, LCS length is 0.

solution.approach: We use the classic dynamic programming solution for the Longest Common Subsequence. Let dp[i][j] be the length of the LCS of the prefixes S[0..i-1] and T[0..j-1]. The recurrence is: if S[i-1]==T[j-1] then dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1 else dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j],dp[i][j-1]). We fill the table row by row, which takes O(|S|*|T|) time. The answer for each test case is dp[|S|][|T|].
